result:
I've investigated this thoroughly. Reporting:

**Root cause**: This is not a recently-introduced bug — it is the known Phase E coroutine stub firing as designed. `reference/lua-c/testes/literals.lua:291` calls `coroutine.wrap(function () gen("", len) end)`, which routes through `aux_resume` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:105-119` and returns `b"coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"`. `aux_wrap` then propagates that as a `LuaError::from_value(...)` at line 158-181, which bubbles up to `pcall_k` in the CLI.

**Status of preceding test logic**: All assertions through line 290 pass. The output shows 5 `+` lines exactly as expected:
- Line 143 `print('+')`, Line 203 `print('+')`, plus the 3 inner `print('+')` calls (lines 157, 163, 172) executed by `dostring(prog)` at line 205.

The long-bracket / line-ending / `_G[var1]` / 15000-char-identifier / UTF-8 / `\xHH` / `\z`-skip / `dostring` reuse / `[==[` nested comment blocks (lines 211–277) all complete cleanly. Failure is exactly at line 291.

**Fix**: None applied. Per `PORTING.md §2 #6`, real stackful coroutines via `corosensei` are Phase E scope; `crates/lua-coro/src/lib.rs` is still the empty skeleton. The stub message is intentional and correct. Acceptable-fix categories in the Debug agent brief (missing Phase-B inherent method, wrong codegen offset, wrong VM arity, sibling C function) do not apply — coroutine wrap/yield require the entire stackful-context-switch machinery which is out of scope for a single-shot debug fix.

**State after**: Unchanged. Test still fails at line 291 with the same Phase E stub message. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, pre-existing).
